Replacing the dynamically linked Qt libraries
==============================================

Ruwa is linked dynamically against Qt 6.10.2 (MinGW 64-bit). Ruwa does not
apply integrity checks or technical restrictions that prevent users from
replacing the Qt shared libraries and plugins in the installation folder.

To use a compatible modified Qt build:

1. Keep a backup copy of the Ruwa installation.
2. Build Qt 6.10.2 for Windows x64 with the MinGW ABI and the modules listed in
   THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md. Apply any desired Qt modifications before building.
3. Replace the Qt6*.dll files and the corresponding plugin directories beside
   Ruwa.exe with the compatible files from that build. Preserve the directory
   names used by the original package.
4. Start Ruwa.exe directly and test the modified libraries.

Ruwa and its standard effect plugins are covered-source components; their
source is published with the matching Ruwa release. No cryptographic signature
check in Ruwa rejects user-replaced Qt libraries. Authenticode signatures, when
present, only identify the publisher and do not prevent replacement.
